questions.prompt: Which three extra vowel letters does German add to the alphabet?
questions.explanation: German uses the umlauts ä, ö and ü. They are separate sounds, not decoration.

questions.prompt: In which word is 'ch' the soft sound, as in the pronoun for the speaker?
questions.explanation: After i, e, ä, ö, ü and consonants the ch is soft. After a, o, u and au it is the hard throat sound.

questions.prompt: Which spelling follows the German rule for the sharp s?
questions.explanation: The u here is short, so the word takes ss. Only a long vowel or a diphthong takes ß, as in die Straße.

questions.prompt: Which sentence uses German capitalisation correctly?
questions.explanation: Every noun is capitalised in German, wherever it stands in the sentence: der Hund, einen Hund.

questions.prompt: You are introduced to your new boss. Which question is appropriate?
questions.explanation: Sie is the polite form for adults you do not know. Its verb takes the -en ending: Sie heißen.

questions.prompt: Complete the greeting used in the early part of the day: 'Guten ___!'
questions.explanation: Guten Morgen is the first greeting of the day. The noun keeps its capital letter.

questions.prompt: Write the number 7 as a German word.
questions.explanation: The numbers one to ten run eins, zwei, drei, vier, fünf, sechs, sieben, acht, neun, zehn.

questions.prompt: Complete: 'Ich ___ aus Spanien.' (the verb for saying where you are from)
questions.explanation: Saying your origin: ich komme aus plus the country. The ich form ends in -e.

questions.prompt: Complete: 'Ich ___ zwanzig Jahre alt.'
questions.explanation: Age uses sein: ich bin zwanzig Jahre alt. German does not use haben here.

questions.prompt: Complete: 'Ich ___ einen Bruder.'
questions.explanation: The ich form of haben is habe. Possession of a person or thing uses haben.

questions.prompt: Complete: 'Du ___ zu schnell.' (the verb for driving)
questions.explanation: fahren is a strong verb: the a becomes ä in the du and er forms, du fährst, er fährt.

questions.prompt: Which sentence has correct German word order?
questions.explanation: The conjugated verb is always the second element. If something else opens the sentence, the subject moves behind the verb.

questions.prompt: Complete: 'Ich sehe ___ Mann.'
questions.explanation: sehen takes the accusative, and masculine der becomes den in that case.

questions.prompt: Complete: 'Ich habe ___ Auto.' (I do not own one)
questions.explanation: A noun with ein or with no article is negated with kein. das Auto is neuter, so the form is kein.

questions.prompt: Complete: 'Ich helfe ___ Bruder.'
questions.explanation: helfen is a dative verb, and the possessive follows the same pattern as the article: dem Bruder, meinem Bruder.

questions.prompt: Complete: 'Ich ___ heute arbeiten.' (obligation, I have to)
questions.explanation: The modal for obligation is müssen, ich form muss. Written with ss because the u is short.

questions.prompt: Complete: 'Der Zug fährt um acht Uhr ___.' (the verb means to depart, so its prefix goes to the end)
questions.explanation: In a main clause the separable prefix jumps to the final position, closing the Satzklammer.

questions.prompt: Which version of the weil sentence uses the correct word order?
options: Ich bleibe zu Hause, weil ich krank bin.
Ich bleibe zu Hause, weil ich bin krank.
Ich bleibe zu Hause, weil bin ich krank.
Ich bleibe zu Hause, weil krank ich bin.
questions.answer: Ich bleibe zu Hause, weil ich krank bin.
questions.explanation: weil sends the conjugated verb to the end. und, aber, oder and denn do not change the order.

questions.prompt: Complete: 'Ich ___ nach Hause gegangen.'
questions.explanation: Verbs of movement and change of state build the Perfekt with sein: ich bin gegangen, ich bin gefahren.

questions.prompt: Complete: 'Gestern ___ ich in Berlin.' (simple past of sein)
questions.explanation: sein and haben are normally used in the simple past even in speech: ich war, ich hatte.

questions.prompt: Complete: 'Ich fahre mit ___ Freunden in die Berge.'
questions.explanation: mit always governs the dative, and the dative plural article is den, with an -n on the noun.

questions.prompt: Complete both gaps: 'Ich hänge das Bild an ___ Wand, und jetzt hängt es an ___ Wand.'
questions.explanation: The two way prepositions take the accusative for movement, wohin, and the dative for position, wo. die Wand becomes an die Wand, then an der Wand.

questions.prompt: Complete: 'Das Buch ___ auf dem Tisch.'
questions.explanation: liegen describes the position and takes no object. legen is the action of putting something down and takes an accusative object.

questions.prompt: Complete: 'Ich wasche ___ die Hände.' (reflexive pronoun in the dative)
questions.explanation: When the reflexive verb already has an accusative object, the reflexive pronoun goes into the dative: ich wasche mir die Hände.

questions.prompt: Which sentence places the verb correctly in the subordinate clause?
options: Ich glaube, dass er morgen kommt.
Ich glaube, dass er kommt morgen.
Ich glaube, dass kommt er morgen.
Ich glaube, dass morgen er kommt.
questions.answer: Ich glaube, dass er morgen kommt.
questions.explanation: dass, wenn and ob all send the conjugated verb to the very end of the clause.

questions.prompt: Complete the narrative past: 'Er ___ mir einen Brief.' (verb schreiben)
questions.explanation: The Präteritum is the tense of written narration. Strong verbs change the stem vowel and add no ending in the er form.

questions.prompt: What is the superlative of 'gut'?
questions.explanation: gut is irregular: gut, besser, am besten.

questions.prompt: Complete: 'Der ___ Mann wohnt hier.'
questions.explanation: After a definite article the adjective takes the weak ending, which is -e in the nominative singular.

questions.prompt: Complete: 'Ich trinke ein ___ Bier.' (adjective kalt)
questions.explanation: After ein the adjective carries the signal of the gender itself, so a neuter noun takes -es in the mixed pattern.

questions.prompt: Which sentence is the polite way to order in a café?
questions.explanation: The Konjunktiv II of haben softens a request. Compare könnten Sie and würden Sie.

questions.prompt: Complete: 'Der Mann, ___ ich gestern geholfen habe, ist mein Nachbar.'
questions.explanation: The relative pronoun takes the case its own clause demands. helfen is a dative verb, so the masculine form is dem.

questions.prompt: Complete: 'Die Frau, ___ Auto vor dem Haus steht, wohnt oben.'
questions.explanation: The genitive relative pronoun is dessen for masculine and neuter and deren for feminine and plural.

questions.prompt: Complete: '___ des schlechten Wetters bleiben wir zu Hause.'
questions.explanation: wegen, während, trotz and statt govern the genitive. The other three take the dative.

questions.prompt: Complete with the correct form: 'Ich kenne ___.' (der Student, accusative)
questions.explanation: Nouns of the n-Deklination, such as der Student, der Kollege and der Herr, add -n or -en in every case except the nominative singular.

questions.prompt: Complete: 'Ich habe vor, nächstes Jahr nach München ___.'
questions.explanation: After expressions like ich habe vor, ich versuche and es ist wichtig the infinitive clause ends in zu plus the infinitive.

questions.prompt: Complete: 'Wir fahren im Juli in den Urlaub. Ich freue mich schon ___.'
questions.explanation: sich freuen has the fixed preposition auf. When it points to a thing rather than a person, the preposition merges into a da-compound.

questions.prompt: Complete: 'Das Haus ___ 1920 gebaut.'
questions.explanation: The passive is werden plus the past participle. In the simple past the singular form is wurde.

questions.prompt: Complete: 'Wenn ich mehr Zeit gehabt hätte, ___ ich gekommen.' (auxiliary for the past Konjunktiv II)
questions.explanation: The past Konjunktiv II uses hätte or wäre plus the participle, and verbs of movement take wäre.

questions.prompt: Complete: '___ es stark regnete, gingen wir spazieren.'
questions.explanation: obwohl is a subordinating conjunction and pushes the verb to the end. trotzdem is an adverb and takes the first position with the verb second.

questions.prompt: Complete: 'Nachdem er gegessen ___, ging er sofort ins Bett.'
questions.explanation: After nachdem the earlier action stands one step further back, so a past main clause pairs with the Plusquamperfekt.
